frameworks.name: compressed sensing
frameworks.one_line: sparse stimulus reconstruction from compressed downstream neural activity.

frameworks.name: linear neuronal response model
frameworks.one_line: exploits widespread linearity of single-neuron responses to ramped inputs.

frameworks.name: hierarchical / multi-layer cortical encoding
frameworks.one_line: compressive and expansive pathways across downstream sensory layers.

core_question: Can downstream neuronal networks preserve and reconstruct sparse stimulus information despite compressive re-encoding across layers?
key_assumptions: individual neuronal responses are approximately linear under sufficiently strong ramped inputs
downstream layers receive compressed signals from upstream layers
manipulations: structure of layer connectivity (compressive vs expansive pathways)
sparsity and form of input stimuli
neuronal network size across downstream layers
key_findings: downstream networks can encode stimulus inputs from their own internal activity
stimuli can be reconstructed even when upstream layers compress information
linearity of neuronal responses to ramped inputs supports this reconstruction
interpretations: information need not be lost across compressive cortical pathways
compressed-sensing-style reconstruction offers a mechanism for cross-layer information preservation
